channel.title: Chart Breakers & Star Stories
channel.itunes:author: Family TVR
channel.itunes:summary: Did your favorite artist just break a record or a heart? The charts are screaming, and we have the inside story you won't hear anywhere else.
channel.description: The latest music trends, chart updates, and star stories from Billboard and beyond. Stay updated with the Pop Culture.

item.title: Ella Langley & Morgan Wallen’s ‘I Can’t Love You Anymore’ Duet Has Arrived: Stream It Now
item.description:
[Intro music plays]
Host: "Welcome to our podcast, where we dive into the latest music trends. Today, we're talking about a exciting new collaboration between two country superstars, Morgan Wallen and Ella Langley. They've just released a duet called 'I Can't Love You Anymore', and we're here to give you the scoop."
[Short music transition]
Host: "The two artists debuted their song with a performance during Wallen's stadium show in Tuscaloosa, Alabama, on April 18th. Wallen told the crowd that Langley wrote the song and sent it to him about a month ago, and he loved it. The song was officially released on April 24th."
[Short music transition]
Host: "Ella Langley may have hinted at the collaboration in her music video for 'Choosin' Texas'. At the end of the video, she rides away in a vehicle with a Tennessee license plate, which features the initials 'ICLYA' - a clear reference to the song title 'I Can't Love You Anymore'. Fan sleuths were quick to pick up on the clue."
[Short music transition]
Host: "Both artists are no strangers to success. Langley's album 'Dandelion' debuted at number one on the Billboard 200 chart, and her song 'Choosin' Texas' has spent seven weeks at the top of the Hot 100 chart. Wallen has had four number one hits on the Hot 100 chart, including 'Last Night', which stayed at the top for 16 weeks in 2023."
[Short music transition]
Host: "Ella Langley will be joining Morgan Wallen on several tour stops this year, and they've already performed together on stage. You can stream their new duet 'I Can't Love You Anymore' now. That's all for today - thanks for tuning in!"
[Outro music plays]
